text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a build of the software. This is followed by running a series of tests to check that the new changes work correctly with the existing code. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. If a test fails, the team is immediately notified, allowing them to fix the issue quickly. This process helps maintain software quality and makes collaboration smoother.
